225	%     [J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)] :
226	%     (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal :=
227	%   .mk' _ (fun f g hf hg ↦ by
228	%     simp only [hP.W_iff (A := A)] at hf hg ⊢
229	%     intro Φ
230	%     rw [Functor.Monoidal.map_tensor]
231	%     infer_instance)"
232	\textit{Source: [Mathlib],
233	\texttt{ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.isMonoidal\_W}, in
234	\texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean}, L48--L57.}
235	This is the \emph{template} for the proof technique used in route~(e), but it does
236	\emph{not} apply directly to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R\): modules over a
237	\emph{varying} ring are not of the form \(C^{op} \to A\) for a fixed monoidal
238	\(A\).
239	
240	\paragraph{The gap and route~(e), superseded.} The substrate
241	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\) of this chapter is the
242	sheafification of \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\), and
243	sheafification of presheaves of modules is exactly the localization
244	\(L = \mathtt{sheafification}\) at the locally-bijective class \(W = J.W\). Route~(e)
245	proposed to obtain a \emph{full} monoidal structure on
246	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\)
247	by applying \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) (ii) to the already-monoidal category (i),
248	which would require the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (its two
249	whiskering-stability fields \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}, with load-bearing
250	local-injectivity half \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}). This is
251	\emph{not} how the group law is realized. The group law consumes only the
252	\emph{existence} of the three coherence isomorphisms, and the associator is built
253	directly by gluing canonical local isomorphisms through
254	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}); it never invokes
255	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), a coherent monoidal category, or the stalkwise
256	whiskering apparatus. The route~(e) instance is therefore vestigial and off the
257	critical path.
258	
259	The stalk-infrastructure analysis that route~(e) turned on is recorded here for
260	reference only, since that route is superseded. The \emph{stalk module structure}
261	is already present in Mathlib: the file
262	\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk} supplies, for a topological
263	space \(X\), a presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) on \(X\) and a point
264	\(x \in X\), the \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-module structure on the stalk
265	\(\mathtt{stalk}\,M.\mathtt{presheaf}\,x\) of a presheaf of modules \(M\),
266	together with the germ--scalar compatibility \(\mathtt{germ\_smul}\); a linearity
267	packaging of the induced stalk map of a morphism was built project-side
268	(\cref{lem:stalk_linear_map}, the \emph{linearity half} of ingredient (d.1)).
269	Route~(e) additionally required a (d.1)-\emph{bridge} between the site-level
270	\(J.W\) and the topological stalkwise-isomorphism criterion on
271	\(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\), and (d.2) the commutation \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x
272	\otimes_{R_x} B_x\) of the stalk with the relative module-presheaf tensor; there is
273	moreover no monoidal \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\) in Mathlib. Because the group law
274	is realized through the direct-gluing associator (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso},
275	via \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}) and never through
276	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), none of this stalk apparatus is on the critical path;
277	it is retained only to document the superseded route.
278	
279	The project's policy on this gap is \emph{project-side}: the substrate is
280	built inside \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/} (the file
281	\(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}\) is its home)
282	and the consumer instance is closed against it. A parallel Mathlib upstream
283	PR with the same content is welcome but not on the critical path for the
284	project; the consumer is closed against the project-side substrate
285	independently of any upstream timing.
286	
287	\section{The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)}
288	\label{sec:tensorobj_substrate}
289	
290	\begin{definition}
291	
292	\leanok
293	[The substrate operation $\otimes$ on $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$]
294	  \label{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
295	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
296	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. For
297	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), define
298	  \[
299	    M \otimes_X N \;\;\in\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
300	  \]
301	  as follows. Choose an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}_{i \in I}\)
302	  of \(X\). On each \(U_i\), the restriction
303	  \(M|_{U_i}\) corresponds (via the equivalence between
304	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A_i}\)-modules and \(A_i\)-modules) to an
305	  \(A_i\)-module \(M_i\), and similarly
306	  \(N|_{U_i}\) corresponds to an \(A_i\)-module \(N_i\). Define
307	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\) as the sheafification of the presheaf
308	  \(U_i \mapsto M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i\). On overlaps
309	  \(U_i \cap U_j = \Spec A_{ij}\) the resulting modules agree via the
310	  canonical compatibility
311	  \((M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i) \otimes_{A_i} A_{ij}
312	     \;\cong\;
313	     (M_j \otimes_{A_j} N_j) \otimes_{A_j} A_{ij}\),
314	  so the data \(\{(M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\}\) glue to a single object
315	  \(M \otimes_X N\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The result is
316	  canonically independent of the affine cover chosen.
317	
318	  Equivalently: the underlying presheaf of \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
319	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product
320	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) of the underlying
321	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\), composed with the sheafification
322	  functor on the small Zariski site of \(X\). The substrate operation
323	  \(\otimes_X\) is, by construction, a \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
324	  object reflecting the relative tensor product of
325	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described in Stacks tag 03DM.
326	\end{definition}
327	
328	\begin{lemma}
329	
330	\leanok
331	[Functoriality of $\otimes_X$]
332	  \label{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
333	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality}
334	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
335	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The assignment
336	  \((M, N) \mapsto M \otimes_X N\) of
337	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} extends to a bifunctor
338	  \[
339	    \otimes_X \;\;\colon\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\times\;
340	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\;\longrightarrow\;\;
341	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
342	  \]
343	  natural in both arguments: a pair of morphisms
344	  \(f : M \to M'\) and \(g : N \to N'\) determines a morphism
345	  \(f \otimes g : M \otimes_X N \to M' \otimes_X N'\) compatible with
346	  identities and composition. This bifunctorial action on morphisms is
347	  the only datum the present lemma provides. The further coherence data
348	  of a monoidal structure --- the unitors
349	  \[
350	    \lambda_M \;\colon\; \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
351	    \qquad
352	    \rho_M \;\colon\; M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
353	  \]
354	  the associator
355	  \(\alpha_{M, N, P} : (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim}
356	   M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\), and the braiding
357	  \(\beta_{M, N} : M \otimes_X N \xrightarrow{\sim} N \otimes_X M\) ---
358	  are \emph{not} outputs of this lemma; they are off the critical path,
359	  recorded in \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
360	\end{lemma}
361	
362	\begin{proof}
363	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
364	  Pick an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}\) of \(X\). On each
365	  affine, the assignment is the standard tensor product of modules over a
366	  commutative ring \(A_i\), which is bifunctorial. The bifunctorial action
367	  on morphisms glues across the cover because the underlying gluing data of
368	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} respect the canonical morphisms on
369	  overlaps; equivalently, the morphism action is inherited from
370	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) (Mathlib module
371	  \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}) under
372	  sheafification. The unitor/associator/braiding coherence data are
373	  \emph{not} constructed here; they are off the critical path
374	  (\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}).
375	\end{proof}
376	
377	\begin{remark}
378	[The full monoidal category on all of $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$ is off-path; the group law consumes only propositions]
379	  \label{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}
380	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
381	        lem:whisker_of_W}
382	  One could ask for the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
383	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, together with
384	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), \(\alpha\), \(\lambda\), \(\rho\) and \(\beta\), to
385	  assemble into a full symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
386	  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), discharging the pentagon,
387	  triangle and hexagon coherence identities. This is explicitly \emph{off-path}
388	  and \emph{not pursued}. One could in principle obtain it as the output of
389	  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) applied to the
390	  already-monoidal presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)
391	  and the sheafification localizer \(J.W\), supplying the instance
392	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}); but the group law never
393	  consumes a coherent monoidal category, so this construction is superseded and not
394	  carried out. The substrate provides only the \emph{existence} of the three
395	  coherence isomorphisms that the group law actually consumes.
396	
397	  Were that superseded route pursued, the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\)
398	  would need \emph{no} closed structure: its
399	  \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) field \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft g)\) for an
400	  \emph{arbitrary} module \(F\) is the flatness-free stalkwise statement
401	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} (a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a
402	  stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes
403	  g_x\) is again an isomorphism for any \(F\)), the same technique Mathlib uses for
404	  fixed-base presheaves in
405	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\). It would use neither
406	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) nor the
407	  sectionwise-flatness hypothesis of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (which is
408	  false over a non-affine open and is likewise off the critical path). But this
409	  apparatus is not on the critical path at all: the group law is realized without
410	  it.
411	
412	  Independently of how the monoidal structure is built, the relative Picard
413	  group law of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is a structure on
414	  \emph{isomorphism classes} of line bundles, and every group axiom there is a
415	  \emph{proposition} of the form \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\).
416	  Such a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it, so the
417	  coherence data of the monoidal category --- pentagon, triangle, hexagon, and
418	  a fortiori any monoidal-closed structure --- is never \emph{consumed} by the
419	  group law. The group law needs only the existence of the three coherence
420	  isomorphisms \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,%
421	  lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, supplied directly: the associator by gluing canonical
422	  local isomorphisms through the restriction-compatibility isomorphism
423	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), and the
424	  unitors and braiding by the cheap \(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern
425	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}). The carrier --- iso-classes
426	  of the substrate tensor \(\otimes_X\) --- is the scheme-theoretic analogue of
427	  Mathlib's Picard group \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R := \mathrm{Skeleton}(
428	  \mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}), but it
429	  is built \emph{differently}: Mathlib reads its \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) off the
430	  \(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) of the coherent monoidal category
431	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalCategory}(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) over a fixed ring,
432	  whereas here --- with no coherent monoidal category on
433	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) available for the varying structure sheaf --- the
434	  group is assembled by hand from the existence-of-isomorphism facts.
435	\end{remark}
436	
437	\section{The lift through \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)}
438	\label{sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
439	
440	The consumer in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} works not with
441	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\) directly but with the subtype
442	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) of locally-trivial
443	modules on the fiber product \(C \times_k T\), introduced in
444	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. This section records the
445	\emph{existence-of-isomorphism facts on line bundles} (associativity, unit,
446	commutativity and inverse) that the relative Picard group law consumes. These facts
447	are supplied \emph{directly}: associativity by the direct-gluing associator
448	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (through the linchpin
449	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), unit and commutativity by the cheap
450	\(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}),
451	and the inverse existentially from \(\otimes\)-invertibility. They are \emph{not}
452	read off any \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure: the route that would derive them from
453	\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal} (route~(e)) is superseded and off-path. The section also
454	records the closure of \(\otimes\) on the locally-trivial
455	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\) subtype (\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}) and
456	the interaction with the pullback functor \(\pi_T^*\). The open-immersion
457	restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is on the
458	critical path precisely because it is consumed by the direct-gluing associator
459	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}; it is \emph{not} tied to the superseded
460	whiskering-stability lemma \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, which belongs
461	to the off-path route~(e).
462	
463	\begin{lemma}
464	
465	\leanok
466	[Sectionwise lax-monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\)]
467	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal}
468	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal}
469	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
470	  % NOTE: this CommRingCat-level lax-monoidal instance
471	  % (`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`, with helpers
472	  % `restrictScalarsLaxε`, `restrictScalarsLaxμ`) is a self-contained
473	  % supplement. It is NOT used by `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, whose proof
474	  % (open-immersion sectionwise base change) identifies restriction along an
475	  % open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
476	  % needs no lax-monoidal pushforward. Retained for potential reuse; off the
477	  % critical path for the tensor group law.
478	  Let \(\varphi : R \to S\) be a morphism of presheaves of \emph{commutative}
479	  rings on a site (so that both \(R\) and \(S\) factor through
480	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and the category of presheaves of modules over each
481	  is monoidal). Then the presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor
482	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) is lax monoidal.
483	  Consequently, composing with the already-monoidal Mathlib functor
484	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) via
485	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\), the full pushforward
486	  \[
487	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi
488	      \;\;:=\;\;
489	    \mathtt{pushforward}_0\,F\,R \;\circ\; \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi
490	  \]
491	  is lax monoidal. This lemma is a self-contained
492	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-level lax-monoidal supplement: it stands on its own
493	  and is retained for potential reuse, but it is \emph{off the critical path}
494	  for the tensor group law. In particular it is \emph{not} an ingredient of
495	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, whose proof identifies restriction along an
496	  open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
497	  consequently needs no lax-monoidal structure on the pushforward.
498	\end{lemma}
499	
500	\begin{proof}
501	
502	  This is a \emph{sectionwise} lift of the existing fact that, for a ring map
503	  \(f : R \to S\), the module restriction-of-scalars functor
504	  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,f\) is lax monoidal
505	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction}, where it is
506	  obtained as the \(\mathtt{rightAdjointLaxMonoidal}\) of the
507	  extension/restriction adjunction \(\mathtt{extendRestrictScalarsAdj}\) ---
508	  i.e.\ Mathlib already uses the same mate idiom one level down). The presheaf
509	  monoidal structure is computed sectionwise,
510	  \((M_1 \otimes M_2).\mathrm{obj}\,X = M_1.\mathrm{obj}\,X \otimes M_2.\mathrm{obj}\,X\),
511	  and the presheaf functor \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) acts
512	  sectionwise as \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{app}\,X)\).
513	  Hence the lax structure maps \(\mu\) (laxity) and \(\varepsilon\) (unit)
514	  assemble from the per-section \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) ones, the required
515	  naturality squares being the naturality of the sectionwise maps against the
516	  presheaf restriction morphisms. Packaging these as a
517	  \(\mathtt{Functor.CoreLaxMonoidal}\) yields the lax-monoidal structure; no
518	  new diagram chase is needed beyond transporting the sectionwise coherence.
519	  The composite \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) is then lax monoidal by
520	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\) together with the monoidality of
521	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) (Mathlib), modulo a defeq
522	  transport across the definitional unfolding of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\).
523	
524	  The commutativity hypothesis is essential: the presheaf-of-modules monoidal
525	  category requires \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-valued presheaves of rings, so the
526	  instance must be stated over \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-factored \(R, S\). In
527	  the project this holds: the relevant \(\varphi\) is
528	  \((\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\), which is
529	  structure-sheaf-valued and hence commutative-ring-valued.
530	\end{proof}
531	
532	\begin{lemma}
533	
534	\leanok
535	[Tensor product commutes with restriction along an open immersion]
536	  \label{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
537	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_restrict_iso}
538	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
539	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, let \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be
540	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, and let
541	  \(f : U \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion, with \((-)|_f\) the
542	  restriction of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along \(f\). The canonical comparison
543	  morphism
544	  \[
545	    (M \otimes_X N)\big|_f
546	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\;}\;
547	    M\big|_f \;\otimes_U\; N\big|_f
548	  \]
549	  is an isomorphism. No local-freeness, flatness, or line-bundle hypothesis on
550	  \(M, N\) is required: the statement holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
551	  The reason is structural to open immersions --- restriction along \(f\) is
552	  base change along the structure-sheaf \emph{isomorphism} induced by \(f\),
553	  not along a general ring map --- and base change along a ring isomorphism is
554	  trivially invertible and commutes with tensor products.
555	\end{lemma}
556	
557	\begin{proof}
558	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
559	  Write \(\varphi\) for the morphism of structure-sheaf-valued presheaves of
560	  rings induced by the open immersion \(f\) (concretely
561	  \(\varphi = (\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\)), so that
562	  restriction along \(f\) is computed by the inverse-image (pullback) functor on
563	  presheaves of modules. The proof exploits the defining feature of an open
564	  immersion: the comparison of structure-sheaf rings over the image is an
565	  \emph{isomorphism}, not merely a ring map. For an open \(V \subseteq U\) the
566	  section ring \(\Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr)\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) over the image
567	  of \(V\) is identified with \(\Gamma(U, V)\) by the canonical \emph{ring
568	  isomorphism}
569	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V : \Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr) \xrightarrow{\sim}
570	  \Gamma(U, V)\)
571	  (the \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) isomorphism
572	  \(\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.appIso}\)). Restriction along \(f\) is
573	  therefore extension of scalars along
574	  this ring isomorphism, and the proof needs no flatness anywhere.
575	
576	  \emph{Step 1 (reduce restriction to the abstract pullback).} The restriction
577	  \((-)|_f\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along the open immersion \(f\) is
578	  naturally isomorphic to the abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback
579	  \((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(-)\), via the
580	  present comparison \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\). It
581	  therefore suffices to produce the comparison and prove it an isomorphism for
582	  the abstract pullback functor.
583	
584	  \emph{Step 2 (move the pullback inside the sheafification).} The substrate
585	  \(\otimes_X\) is sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
586	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}). The pullback commutes with
587	  sheafification: there is a natural isomorphism
588	  \(\mathtt{sheafification} \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
589	   \;\cong\;
590	   \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi \circ \mathtt{sheafification}\)
591	  (the present \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\)). Hence the
592	  whole question descends to the presheaf level, where it is the base-change
593	  comparison
594	  \[
595	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(A \otimes B)
596	      \;\longrightarrow\;
597	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A
598	      \;\otimes\;
599	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B
600	  \]
601	  for the underlying presheaves of modules \(A, B\) of \(M\) and \(N\).
602	
603	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
604	  % \ref{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback} (the pair $(f^*, f_*)$ of
605	  % pullback/pushforward of $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a morphism of ringed
606	  % spaces is an adjoint pair)
607	  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L252-273).
608	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
609	  % "\begin{lemma}
610	  % \label{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback}
611	  % Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$
612	  % be a morphism of ringed spaces.
613	  % \begin{enumerate}
614	  % \item The functor
615	  % $f_* : \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \to \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_Y)$
616	  % is left exact. In fact it commutes with all limits.
617	  % \item The functor
618	  % $f^* : \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_Y) \to \textit{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)$
619	  % is right exact. In fact it commutes with all colimits.
620	  %  [\dots]
621	  % \end{enumerate}
622	  % \end{lemma}
623	  %  [\dots]
624	  % Parts (1) and (2) hold because $(f^*, f_*)$ is an adjoint pair
625	  % of functors, see
626	  % Sheaves, Lemma \ref{sheaves-lemma-adjoint-pullback-pushforward-modules}"
627	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
628	  \texttt{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback}: pullback and pushforward of
629	  $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a morphism form an adjoint pair $(f^*, f_*)$, the
630	  abstract universal property on which the comparison below rests.}
631	
632	  \emph{Step 3 (the genuine residual: H1 alone).} Completing this comparison at the
633	  presheaf level requires a sectionwise handle on the abstract functor
634	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\), which is defined as the left
635	  adjoint of pushforward (the presheaf-of-modules avatar of the
636	  \(f^* \dashv f_*\) adjunction of the cited Stacks lemma) and admits no
637	  sectionwise formula in Mathlib at the pinned commit. The closure passes through
638	  two presheaf-level ingredients, of which exactly one now remains open.
639	
640	  The first ingredient --- a strong-monoidal upgrade of
641	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along the structure-sheaf ring \emph{isomorphism}
642	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\), so that base change along the isomorphism commutes with
643	  \(\otimes\) --- is \emph{closed in this chapter} as
644	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
645	  (\(\mathtt{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}\)): for a ring isomorphism
646	  \(e : R \simeq S\) the canonical map \(a \otimes_R b \mapsto a \otimes_S b\) is an
647	  \(R\)-linear equivalence \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R
648	  \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M
649	  \otimes_S N)\).
650	
651	  The \emph{sole remaining residual} is therefore the presheaf-level identification
652	  \[
653	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;\cong\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
654	  \]
655	  via \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\). One exhibits a
656	  presheaf-level adjunction
657	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) and concludes
658	  that \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\), being a left adjoint of
659	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\), is canonically isomorphic to the abstract left
660	  adjoint \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\). Here \(\beta\) is the structure map of the
661	  restriction functor, so that \((M|_f).\mathrm{val} =
662	  (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,M.\mathrm{val}\) definitionally;
663	  composing the resulting comparison with
664	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv} yields the asserted isomorphism.
665	
666	  This presheaf-level adjunction is the presheaf analogue of the sheaf-level
667	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\), and must be built from
668	  presheaf-level \(\mathtt{pushforwardNatTrans}\) and
669	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardCongr}\). These two building blocks are \emph{Mathlib-absent}
670	  at the pinned commit: only the \emph{sheaf}-level versions exist
671	  (\texttt{Mathlib/.../SheafOfModules/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean}), whereas
672	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardId}\) and \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) do already exist at the
673	  presheaf level. This residual is being constructed \emph{directly}, mirroring the
674	  existing sheaf-level template app-for-app at the presheaf level (a Mathlib-gradient
675	  build, not a deferral to an upstream PR).
676	
677	  This lemma is the single substrate linchpin of the relative Picard group law for
678	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(M, N\): the associator
679	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} consumes the comparison
680	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_f \cong M|_f \otimes_U N|_f\) on its sole consumer
681	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}, where the restriction is commuted
682	  past the \emph{sheafified} tensor \(M \otimes_X N\) \emph{before} any local
683	  trivialisation of \(M, N\) is introduced --- so the comparison is required for the
684	  global, untrivialised modules, and no free-cover specialisation can stand in for it.
685	  Consequently the H1 residual \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong
686	  \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) is genuinely on the critical path, and closing it is
687	  the \emph{single open substrate obligation} of this chapter. The superseded
688	  route~(e) \((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal} \to \mathtt{Sheaf.monoidalCategory}\)
689	  (\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}) remains named only as a last-resort fallback should the
690	  presheaf-level adjunction itself bottom out; it is not the plan.
691	\end{proof}
692	
693	\begin{lemma}
694	[Presheaf-level pushforward adjunction and strong-monoidal restriction (H1/H2 substrate)]
695	  \label{lem:presheaf_pushforward_adj_substrate}
696	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans,
697	        PresheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr,
698	        PresheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj,
699	        PresheafOfModules.isIso_of_isIso_app,
700	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}
701	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
702	  These five presheaf-level declarations are the two ingredients on which the
703	  linchpin \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} rests; both are absent from Mathlib at
704	  the pinned commit and are supplied project-side.
705	
706	  \emph{H1 (the pushforward adjunction).} For a morphism \(\beta\) of presheaves of
707	  rings, \(\mathtt{pushforwardNatTrans}\) and \(\mathtt{pushforwardCongr}\) are the
708	  presheaf-level de-sheafifications of Mathlib's sheaf-level
709	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatTrans}\) and
710	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr}\)
711	  (\texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean}),
712	  reproduced app-for-app one level down at the presheaf of modules. From them
713	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\) assembles the presheaf-level adjunction
714	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\), the
715	  presheaf analogue of the sheaf-level
716	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\). Pairing this against the
717	  existing \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\) through
718	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) yields the canonical isomorphism
719	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) of two left
720	  adjoints of the same pushforward --- exactly the residual identified in Step~3 of
721	  the proof of \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}.
722	
723	  \emph{H2 (strong-monoidal restriction).} \(\mathtt{isIso\_of\_isIso\_app}\) is the
724	  sectionwise isomorphism criterion: a morphism of presheaves of modules is an
725	  isomorphism once each of its section maps is. Built on it,
726	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}\) equips the presheaf-level
727	  restriction-of-scalars functor
728	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\alpha\) with a \emph{strong}-monoidal
729	  structure whenever the base-change comparison \(\alpha\) is \emph{sectionwise
730	  bijective} --- the presheaf-level lift of the ModuleCat-level H2 core
731	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}, assembled section-by-section via
732	  \(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.ofLaxMonoidal}\). It is to be distinguished from the
733	  module-level, ring-equivalence form
734	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv}\) already pinned in
735	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}: the present declaration is the
736	  \emph{presheaf}-level, \emph{bijective}-comparison form, the one the substrate lift
737	  actually consumes (where the comparison map
738	  \((\alpha.\mathtt{app}\,X).\mathtt{hom}\) is a bijective \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)
739	  morphism but is not syntactically a \((\,\cdot\,)\!.\mathtt{toRingHom}\)).

742	  are self-contained presheaf-level results and are natural upstream-PR candidates.
743	\end{lemma}

745	\begin{lemma}
746	
747	\leanok
748	[Base change along a ring isomorphism commutes with $\otimes$]
749	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
750	  \lean{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}
751	  Let \(e : R \simeq S\) be an isomorphism of commutative rings, and let \(M, N\) be
752	  \(S\)-modules. Restriction of scalars along \(e\) commutes with the tensor
753	  product: the canonical map
754	  \[
755	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N)
756	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
757	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\bigl(M \otimes_S N\bigr),
758	    \qquad a \otimes_R b \;\longmapsto\; a \otimes_S b,
759	  \]
760	  is an \(R\)-linear equivalence. Equivalently, \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\) is
761	  \emph{strong} monoidal, not merely lax, when \(e\) is a ring \emph{isomorphism}:
762	  base change along an isomorphism \(R \simeq S\) identifies the two ground rings, so
763	  the lax structure map of \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\) is invertible. The
764	  forward map is \(R\)-linear, but its inverse \(a \otimes_S b \mapsto a \otimes_R b\)
765	  is only \emph{additive} (it is not \(S\)-linear), so the inverse is constructed as
766	  a homomorphism of additive groups and then verified to be the two-sided inverse of
767	  the forward \(R\)-linear map. This closes the strong-monoidal ingredient of
768	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, leaving the presheaf-level pushforward
769	  adjunction as the sole open residual there.
770	\end{lemma}
771	
772	\begin{lemma}
773	
774	[Strong-monoidal upgrade of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along a ring isomorphism (ModuleCat-level H2 core)]
775	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_strongmonoidal}

1826	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Mathlib).
1827	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f).app (tensorObj M N) ≪≫ ?_
1828	  -- Step 2. **Sheafification commutes with pullback.** `tensorObj M N` is, by
1829	  -- definition, `sheafification.obj (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj

1923	  exact tensorObj_restrict_iso W.ι M N ≪≫
1924	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU eM) (restrictIsoUnitOfLE hWU' eN) ≪≫

1512	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
1513	
1514	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
1515	
1516	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
1517	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
1518	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
1519	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
1520	
1521	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
1522	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
1523	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1524	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
1525	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
1526	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
1527	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
1528	
1529	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
1530	
1531	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
1532	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
1533	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
1534	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
1535	
1536	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
1537	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
1538	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
1539	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
1540	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
1541	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
1542	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
1543	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
1544	
1545	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
1546	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
1547	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
1548	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
1549	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
1550	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
1551	law is definitional. -/
1552	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1553	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
1554	
1555	/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^∨ := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)`** of an `𝒪_X`-module.
1556	
1557	For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
1558	sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the

/-!
# Sheaves of modules over a sheaf of rings

In this file, we define the category `SheafOfModules R` when `R : Sheaf J RingCat`
is a sheaf of rings on a category `C` equipped with a Grothendieck topology `J`.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe v v₁ u₁ u w

open CategoryTheory

variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
  (R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u})

/-- A sheaf of modules is a presheaf of modules such that the underlying presheaf
of abelian groups is a sheaf. -/
structure SheafOfModules where
  /-- the underlying presheaf of modules of a sheaf of modules -/
  val : PresheafOfModules.{v} R.obj
  isSheaf : Presheaf.IsSheaf J val.presheaf

namespace SheafOfModules

variable {R}

/-- A morphism between sheaves of modules is a morphism between the underlying
presheaves of modules. -/
@[ext]
structure Hom (X Y : SheafOfModules.{v} R) where
  /-- a morphism between the underlying presheaves of modules -/
  val : X.val ⟶ Y.val

instance : Category (SheafOfModules.{v} R) where
  Hom := Hom
  id _ := ⟨𝟙 _⟩
  comp f g := ⟨f.val ≫ g.val⟩

@[ext]
lemma hom_ext {X Y : SheafOfModules.{v} R} {f g : X ⟶ Y} (h : f.val = g.val) : f = g :=
  Hom.ext h

@[simp]
lemma id_val (X : SheafOfModules.{v} R) : Hom.val (𝟙 X) = 𝟙 X.val := rfl

@[simp, reassoc]
lemma comp_val {X Y Z : SheafOfModules.{v} R} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
    (f ≫ g).val = f.val ≫ g.val := rfl

variable (R)
/-- The forgetful functor `SheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules R.val`. -/
@[simps]
def forget : SheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules R.obj where
  obj F := F.val
  map φ := φ.val

/-- The forget functor `SheafOfModules R ⥤ PresheafOfModules R.val` is fully faithful. -/
@[simps]
def fullyFaithfulForget : (forget.{v} R).FullyFaithful where
  preimage φ := ⟨φ⟩

instance : (forget.{v} R).Faithful := (fullyFaithfulForget R).faithful

instance : (forget.{v} R).Full := (fullyFaithfulForget R).full

instance : (forget.{v} R).ReflectsIsomorphisms := (fullyFaithfulForget R).reflectsIsomorphisms

/-- Evaluation on an object `X` gives a functor
`SheafOfModules R ⥤ ModuleCat (R.val.obj X)`. -/
def evaluation (X : Cᵒᵖ) : SheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ ModuleCat.{v} (R.obj.obj X) :=
  forget _ ⋙ PresheafOfModules.evaluation _ X

/-- The forget functor `SheafOfModules R ⥤ Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat`. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def toSheaf : SheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{v} where
  obj M := ⟨_, M.isSheaf⟩
  map f := { hom := (forget R ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R.obj).map f }

/--
The forgetful functor from sheaves of modules over sheaf of ring `R` to sheaves of `R(X)`-module
when `X` is initial.
-/
@[simps]
noncomputable def forgetToSheafModuleCat
      (X : Cᵒᵖ) (hX : Limits.IsInitial X) :
    SheafOfModules.{w} R ⥤ Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{w} (R.1.obj X)) where
  obj M := ⟨(PresheafOfModules.forgetToPresheafModuleCat X hX).obj M.1,
    Presheaf.isSheaf_of_isSheaf_comp _ _
      (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{w} (R.1.obj X)) AddCommGrpCat.{w}) M.isSheaf⟩
  map f := { hom := (PresheafOfModules.forgetToPresheafModuleCat X hX).map f.1 }

/-- The canonical isomorphism between
`SheafOfModules.toSheaf R ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{v}`
and `SheafOfModules.forget R ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R.val`. -/
noncomputable def toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso :
    toSheaf R ⋙ sheafToPresheaf J AddCommGrpCat.{v} ≅
      forget R ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R.obj := Iso.refl _

instance : (toSheaf.{v} R).Faithful :=
  Functor.Faithful.of_comp_iso (toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso.{v} R)

instance (M N : SheafOfModules.{v} R) : AddCommGroup (M ⟶ N) :=
  (fullyFaithfulForget R).homEquiv.addCommGroup

@[simp]
lemma add_val {M N : SheafOfModules.{v} R} (f g : M ⟶ N) :

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Localization

/-!
# The category of sheaves of modules as a localization of presheaves of modules

Similarly as the category of sheaves with values in a category identify to
a localization of the category of presheaves with respect to those morphisms
which become isomorphisms after sheafification
(see the file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Localization.lean`), we show that
the sheafification functor from presheaves of modules to sheaves of modules
is a localization functor.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe v u v' u'

open CategoryTheory

variable {C : Type u'} [Category.{v'} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}

namespace PresheafOfModules

variable {R₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {R : Sheaf J RingCat.{u}} (α : R₀ ⟶ R.obj)
  [Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J α] [Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J α]
  [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective AddCommGrpCat.{v}]
  [HasWeakSheafify J AddCommGrpCat.{v}]

open MorphismProperty in
lemma inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms :
    J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf R₀) = (isomorphisms _).inverseImage (sheafification α) := by
  rw [J.W_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms]
  ext P Q f
  simp only [inverseImage_iff, isomorphisms.iff,
    ← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{v} R)]
  exact (isomorphisms _).arrow_mk_iso_iff
    (((Functor.mapArrowFunctor _ _).mapIso (sheafificationCompToSheaf.{v} α)).app (Arrow.mk f))

instance : (sheafification.{v} α).IsLocalization (J.W.inverseImage (toPresheaf R₀)) := by
  rw [inverseImage_W_toPresheaf_eq_inverseImage_isomorphisms α]
  exact (sheafificationAdjunction.{v} α).isLocalization

end PresheafOfModules

section

variable {F G : Sheaf J A} (f : F ⟶ G) [(forget A).ReflectsIsomorphisms]
  [J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)]

lemma isLocallyBijective_iff_isIso :
    IsLocallyInjective f ∧ IsLocallySurjective f ↔ IsIso f := by
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨_, _⟩
    rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso f (sheafCompose J (forget A)),
      ← isLocallyBijective_iff_isIso']
    constructor <;> infer_instance
  · intro
    constructor <;> infer_instance

end

end Sheaf

variable (J A)

namespace GrothendieckTopology

/-- Given a category `C` equipped with a Grothendieck topology `J` and a concrete category `A`,
this property holds if a morphism in `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` satisfies `J.W` (i.e. becomes an iso after
sheafification) iff it is both locally injective and locally surjective. -/
class WEqualsLocallyBijective : Prop where
  iff {X Y : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : X ⟶ Y) :
    J.W f ↔ Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective J f ∧ Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J f

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

variable {X Y Z T : Scheme.{u}}

variable (X) in
/-- The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme. -/
def Modules := SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf

namespace Modules

/-- Morphisms between `𝒪ₓ`-modules. Use `Hom.app` to act on sections. -/
def Hom (M N : X.Modules) : Type u := SheafOfModules.Hom M N

instance : Category X.Modules where
  Hom := Modules.Hom
  __ := (inferInstance : Category (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf))

noncomputable instance : Abelian X.Modules :=
  inferInstanceAs <| Abelian (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf)
instance : HasLimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasLimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
instance : HasColimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasColimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))

section Functor

variable (X) in
/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules.
This is mostly useful to transport results from (pre)sheaves of modules to `𝒪ₓ`-modules and
usually shouldn't be used directly when working with actual `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
def toPresheafOfModules : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules := SheafOfModules.forget _

/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules is fully faithful. -/
def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _

instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Full := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.full
instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Faithful := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.faithful
instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).IsRightAdjoint :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isRightAdjoint

variable (X) in
/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _

instance : (toPresheaf X).Faithful := .comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
instance : PreservesLimits (toPresheaf X) := comp_preservesLimits _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
instance : (toPresheaf X).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
  reflectsIsomorphisms_comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)

end Functor

variable {M N K : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} {U V : X.Opens}

section Presheaf

/-- The underlying abelian presheaf of an `𝒪ₓ`-module. -/
noncomputable def presheaf (M : X.Modules) : TopCat.Presheaf Ab X := M.1.presheaf

/-- Notation for sections of a presheaf of module. -/
scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation3 "Γ(" M ", " U ")" => (Scheme.Modules.presheaf M).obj (.op U)

instance : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, U) := (M.val.obj (.op U)).isModule

variable (M) in
@[simp] lemma map_smul (i : U ⟶ V) (r : Γ(X, V)) (x : Γ(M, V)) :
    M.presheaf.map i.op (r • x) = X.presheaf.map i.op r • M.presheaf.map i.op x :=
  M.val.map_smul _ _ _

/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
  (toPresheaf X).map φ

/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))

@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl

@[simp]
lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x

@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl

@[ext]
lemma hom_ext (f g : M ⟶ N) (H : ∀ U, f.app U = g.app U) : f = g := by
  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
  ext U x
  exact congr($(H U.unop) x)

lemma isSheaf (M : X.Modules) : M.presheaf.IsSheaf := SheafOfModules.isSheaf M

@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_obj : (toPresheaf X).obj M = M.presheaf := rfl
@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map : (toPresheaf X).map φ = φ.mapPresheaf := rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app {M N : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} :
    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U) := by
  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (toPresheaf X), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
  simp [Opposite.op_surjective.forall]

instance [IsIso φ] : IsIso (φ.app U) := Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mp ‹_› _

@[simp, push ←]
lemma inv_app [IsIso φ] : (inv φ).app U = inv (φ.app U) := by
  apply IsIso.eq_inv_of_hom_inv_id
  simp [← Hom.comp_app]

end Presheaf

noncomputable section Functorial

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T)

/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl

115	def IsLocallyTrivial {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=

def pseudofunctor :
    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=
  LocallyDiscrete.mkPseudofunctor
    (fun X ↦ Adj.mk (Cat.of X.unop.Modules))
    (fun f ↦ .mk (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.unop).toCat)
    (fun _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackId _))
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardId _).symm))
    (fun _ _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackComp _ _).symm)
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardComp _ _)))

end Functorial

noncomputable section Restriction

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g]

/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
but has better defeqs. -/
def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
  (restrictFunctor f).obj M

@[simp] lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp] lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
  · ext U x
    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
    exact congr($this x)

instance : IsIso (restrictAdjunction f).counit :=
  inferInstanceAs (IsIso <| (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso f).hom)

instance : (restrictFunctor f).IsLeftAdjoint := (restrictAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Full := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
instance : (pushforward f).Faithful := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app (M : Y.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).unit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.image_preimage_le U)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_counit_app_app (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).counit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm).op := rfl

/-- Restriction is naturally isomorphic to the inverse image. -/
def restrictFunctorIsoPullback : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f :=
  (restrictAdjunction f).leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)

/-- Restriction along the identity is isomorphic to the identity. -/
def restrictFunctorId : restrictFunctor (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp))) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr
      (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_hom_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.hom.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show U = 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_inv_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.inv.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U = U by simp)).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along the composition is isomorphic to the composition of restrictions. -/
def restrictFunctorComp : restrictFunctor (f ≫ g) ≅ restrictFunctor g ⋙ restrictFunctor f :=
  have : (f.opensFunctor ⋙ g.opensFunctor).IsContinuous
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) :=
    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ (Opens.grothendieckTopology _) _
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp)) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_hom_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_inv_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def restrictFunctorCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] :
    restrictFunctor f ≅ restrictFunctor g :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp [hf])) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; subst hf; simp)

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along open immersions commutes with taking stalks. -/
def restrictStalkNatIso (x : X) :
    restrictFunctor f ⋙ toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x ≅
    toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ (f x) :=
  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
  (toPresheaf _ ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft (OpenNhds (f x))ᵒᵖ Y.Opensᵒᵖ Ab).obj
      (OpenNhds.inclusion (f x)).op).isoWhiskerLeft
      (Functor.Final.colimIso (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op)

@[simp]
lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
    ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU ≫
      (restrictStalkNatIso f x).hom.app M = M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) :=
  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
  Functor.Final.ι_colimitIso_hom
    (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op
    ((OpenNhds.inclusion ((ConcreteCategory.hom f.base) x)).op ⋙ M.presheaf) _

theorem app_bijective_of_stalkFunctor_map_bijective {F G : Sheaf C X} (f : F ⟶ G) (U : Opens X)
    (h : ∀ x ∈ U, Function.Bijective ((stalkFunctor C x).map f.1)) :
    Function.Bijective (f.1.app (op U)) :=
  ⟨app_injective_of_stalkFunctor_map_injective f.1 U fun x hx => (h x hx).1,
    app_surjective_of_stalkFunctor_map_bijective f U h⟩

include instCC in
theorem app_isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso {F G : Sheaf C X} (f : F ⟶ G) (U : Opens X)
    [∀ x : U, IsIso ((stalkFunctor C x.val).map f.1)] : IsIso (f.1.app (op U)) := by
  -- Since the forgetful functor of `C` reflects isomorphisms, it suffices to see that the
  -- underlying map between types is an isomorphism, i.e. bijective.
  suffices IsIso ((forget C).map (f.1.app (op U))) by
    exact isIso_of_reflects_iso (f.1.app (op U)) (forget C)
  rw [isIso_iff_bijective]
  apply app_bijective_of_stalkFunctor_map_bijective
  intro x hx
  apply (bijective_iff_isIso_ofHom _).mpr
  exact Functor.map_isIso (forget C) ((stalkFunctor C (⟨x, hx⟩ : U).1).map f.1)

include instCC in
-- Making this an instance would cause a loop in typeclass resolution with `Functor.map_isIso`
/-- Let `F` and `G` be sheaves valued in a concrete category, whose forgetful functor reflects
isomorphisms, preserves limits and filtered colimits. Then if the stalk maps of a morphism
`f : F ⟶ G` are all isomorphisms, `f` must be an isomorphism.
-/
theorem isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso {F G : Sheaf C X} (f : F ⟶ G)
    [∀ x : X, IsIso ((stalkFunctor C x).map f.1)] : IsIso f := by
  -- Since the inclusion functor from sheaves to presheaves is fully faithful, it suffices to
  -- show that `f`, as a morphism between _presheaves_, is an isomorphism.
  suffices IsIso ((Sheaf.forget C X).map f) by exact isIso_of_fully_faithful (Sheaf.forget C X) f
  -- We show that all components of `f` are isomorphisms.
  suffices ∀ U : (Opens X)ᵒᵖ, IsIso (f.1.app U) by
    exact @NatIso.isIso_of_isIso_app _ _ _ _ F.1 G.1 f.1 this
  intro U; induction U
  apply app_isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso

include instCC in
/-- Let `F` and `G` be sheaves valued in a concrete category, whose forgetful functor reflects
isomorphisms, preserves limits and filtered colimits. Then a morphism `f : F ⟶ G` is an
isomorphism if and only if all of its stalk maps are isomorphisms.
-/
theorem isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso {F G : Sheaf C X} (f : F ⟶ G) :
    IsIso f ↔ ∀ x : X, IsIso ((stalkFunctor C x).map f.1) :=
  ⟨fun _ x =>
    @Functor.map_isIso _ _ _ _ _ _ (stalkFunctor C x) f.1 ((Sheaf.forget C X).map_isIso f),
   fun _ => isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso f⟩

end Concrete

end TopCat.Presheaf

/-- A `TopCat.Sheaf C X` is a presheaf of objects from `C` over a (bundled) topological space `X`,
satisfying the sheaf condition.
-/
nonrec def Sheaf : Type max u v w :=
  Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) C
deriving Category

variable {C X}

/-- The underlying presheaf of a sheaf -/
abbrev Sheaf.presheaf (F : X.Sheaf C) : TopCat.Presheaf C X :=
  F.1

variable (C X)

-- Let's construct a trivial example, to keep the inhabited linter happy.
instance sheafInhabited : Inhabited (Sheaf (CategoryTheory.Discrete PUnit) X) :=
  ⟨⟨Functor.star _, Presheaf.isSheaf_unit _⟩⟩

namespace Sheaf

/-- The forgetful functor from sheaves to presheaves.
-/
def forget : TopCat.Sheaf C X ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf C X :=
  sheafToPresheaf _ _

instance forget_full : (forget C X).Full where

tensorObj_unit_iso

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the d.2-free descent re-route (B-connector)

The "locally-iso ⇒ iso" half of the descent assembly of `exists_tensorObj_inverse`:
a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on an open
neighbourhood of every point is a global isomorphism. The route is the stalkwise
iso criterion `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (for sheaves valued
in `Ab`, whose forgetful functor reflects isos and preserves limits / filtered
colimits) together with `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso` (restriction along an
open immersion commutes with stalks). **No stalk-⊗ ("d.2") is invoked**: this is a
statement about a single module morphism, never about the tensor stalk. -/

/-- **B-connector: a morphism of `𝒪_X`-modules that restricts to an isomorphism on
an open cover is an isomorphism.** For `φ : M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules`, if every point
`x` lies in an open `U x` on which the restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ`
is an isomorphism, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This is the B-bridge of the d.2-free
descent re-route assembling `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
lemma isIso_of_isIso_restrict {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
    (U : X → X.Opens) (hxU : ∀ x, x ∈ U x)
    (h : ∀ x, IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ)) :
    IsIso φ := by
  -- It suffices that each stalk map of the underlying `Ab`-sheaf morphism is iso.
  have hst : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab x).map
      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
    intro x
    obtain ⟨x', hx'⟩ : ∃ x', (U x).ι.base x' = x := by
      have hmem : x ∈ (U x).ι.opensRange := by
        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hxU x
      exact AlgebraicGeometry.IsOpenImmersion.mem_opensRange.mp hmem
    set α := Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).ι x' with hα
    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x
    -- `F.map φ` is iso: a functor preserves the iso `(restrictFunctor _).map φ`.
    haveI hF : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι ⋙ Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf _ ⋙
        TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab x').map φ) := by
      dsimp only [Functor.comp_map]; infer_instance
    -- Naturality of `α` rewrites the stalk-at-`x` map as a composite of isos.
    have key : (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab
          ((U x).ι.base x')).map φ
        = (α.app M).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι ⋙ Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf _ ⋙
            TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab x').map φ ≫ (α.app N).hom := by
      rw [Iso.inv_comp_eq]
      exact (α.hom.naturality φ).symm
    have hG : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab
        ((U x).ι.base x')).map φ) := by rw [key]; infer_instance
    have hG' : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab ((U x).ι.base x')).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := hG
    exact hx' ▸ hG'
  -- Package as a morphism of `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` and apply the stalkwise iso criterion.
  let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
  let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩
  let fS : MS ⟶ NS := ⟨(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ⟩
  haveI : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab x).map fS.val) := hst
  haveI hSiso : IsIso fS := TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso fS
  have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) := by

-- It suffices that each stalk map of the underlying `Ab`-sheaf morphism is iso.
  have hst : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
    intro x
    obtain ⟨x', hx'⟩ : ∃ x', (U x).ι x' = x := by
      have hmem : x ∈ (U x).ι.opensRange := by
        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hxU x
      exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange.mp hmem
    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x
    -- `(restrictFunctor … ⋙ toPresheaf … ⋙ stalkFunctor x').map φ` is iso (functor of an iso).
    haveI hFφ : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι ⋙
        Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x').map φ) := by
      infer_instance
    -- Transport the iso across `restrictStalkNatIso` to the stalk at `(U x).ι x' = x`.
    have hGφ : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} ((U x).ι x')).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) :=
      (CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_map_iff
        (Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).ι x') φ).mp hFφ
    exact hx' ▸ hGφ
  -- Package as a morphism of `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` and apply the stalkwise iso criterion.
  let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
  let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩
  let fS : MS ⟶ NS := ⟨(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ⟩
  haveI : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map fS.hom) := hst
  haveI hSiso : IsIso fS := TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso fS
  have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) := by
    have := (TopCat.Sheaf.forget Ab.{u} X).map_isIso fS
    exact this
  exact (CategoryTheory.isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso φ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)).mp h1

/-- The morphism of presheaves of modules `M₁ ⟶ M₂` given by a morphism
of abelian presheaves `M₁.presheaf ⟶ M₂.presheaf`
which satisfy a suitable linearity condition. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def homMk (φ : M₁.presheaf ⟶ M₂.presheaf)
    (hφ : ∀ (X : Cᵒᵖ) (r : R.obj X) (m : M₁.obj X), φ.app X (r • m) = r • φ.app X m) :
    M₁ ⟶ M₂ where
  app X := ModuleCat.ofHom
    { toFun := φ.app X
      map_add' := by simp +instances
      map_smul' := hφ X }
  naturality := fun f ↦ by
    ext x
    exact CategoryTheory.congr_fun (φ.naturality f) x

instance : Zero (M₁ ⟶ M₂) where
  zero := { app := fun _ ↦ 0 }

variable (M₁ M₂) in
@[simp] lemma zero_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) : (0 : M₁ ⟶ M₂).app X = 0 := rfl

instance : Neg (M₁ ⟶ M₂) where
  neg f :=
    { app := fun X ↦ -f.app X
      naturality := fun {X Y} h ↦ by
        ext x
        simp [← naturality_apply] }

instance : Add (M₁ ⟶ M₂) where
  add f g :=
    { app := fun X ↦ f.app X + g.app X
      naturality := fun {X Y} h ↦ by
        ext x
        simp [← naturality_apply] }

    (fun _ => Sheaf.homEquiv.toIso) (fun _ => rfl)

/-- Given two sheaves `F` and `G` on a site `(C, J)` with values in a category `A`,
this `sheafHom F G` is the sheaf of types which sends an object `X : C`
to the type of morphisms between the "restrictions" of `F` and `G` to the category `Over X`. -/
def sheafHom (F G : Sheaf J A) : Sheaf J (Type _) where
  obj := sheafHom' F G
  property := (Presheaf.isSheaf_of_iso_iff (sheafHom'Iso F G)).2 (G.2.hom F.1)

/-- The sections of the sheaf `sheafHom F G` identify to morphisms `F ⟶ G`. -/
def sheafHomSectionsEquiv (F G : Sheaf J A) :
    (sheafHom F G).1.sections ≃ (F ⟶ G) :=
  ((Functor.sectionsFunctor Cᵒᵖ).mapIso (sheafHom'Iso F G)).toEquiv.trans
    ((presheafHomSectionsEquiv F.1 G.1).trans Sheaf.homEquiv.symm)

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (1, axiom-clean):** `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_of_isIso_restrict`
  (the **B-connector** of the d.2-free descent re-route), lines ~1936–1972.
- **Declarations blocked (0 new sorries):** none added with sorry. The two remaining bridges
  (A-bridge, C) were NOT added — they are genuine multi-iteration builds; handed off precisely
  below rather than pinned as sorries (per plan directive "do NOT pin a sorry into a NEW decl").
- **Sorry count (this file):** before = 3 (L609 vestigial d.2; `exists_tensorObj_inverse`;
  `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj`) → after = 3 (unchanged; B-connector is sorry-free).
- **Ride-along done:** refreshed the stale comment in the `exists_tensorObj_inverse` sorry body
  (now describes the d.2-free descent plan + the landed B-connector, replacing the obsolete
  "dual is absent" text).
- Build is GREEN (no errors); only pre-existing deprecation/long-line/`sorry` warnings remain.

## `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` (B-connector) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement:** for `φ : M ⟶ N` in `X.Modules`, a choice function `U : X → X.Opens` with
  `∀ x, x ∈ U x` and `∀ x, IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ)`, then `IsIso φ`.
  (This is exactly the form `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial` supplies via choice, so the assembly
  can feed it directly.)
- **Approach (worked first serious try):** stalkwise. For each `x`, pick a preimage point
  `x' : ↥(U x)` of `x` under the open immersion `(U x).ι` (via `Scheme.Hom.mem_opensRange` +
  `Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι`). The restriction `(restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ` is iso (hyp),
  so a functor sends it to an iso; transport across `Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).ι x'`
  (restriction commutes with stalks) via `CategoryTheory.NatIso.isIso_map_iff` to deduce that the
  stalk-at-`x` map of `(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ` is iso. Package `M.presheaf`/`N.presheaf`
  as `TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X` (sheaf condition = `M.isSheaf`), apply
  `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso`, then reflect back to `X.Modules` via
  `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf` (`ReflectsIsomorphisms`).
- **Result:** RESOLVED — `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.
- **Key API gotchas (for reuse):**
  - `Ab` needs explicit universe `Ab.{u}` everywhere (else `stalkFunctor` lands at universe 0 and
    mismatches `toPresheaf X`'s `TopCat.Presheaf Ab.{u} X`).
  - `Functor.map_isIso` is NOT an instance through a triple composite; close `IsIso ((A⋙B⋙C).map φ)`
    with `dsimp only [Functor.comp_map]; exact Functor.map_isIso _ _`.
  - `TopCat.Sheaf` hom is an induced-category hom with field `.hom` (NOT `.val`): use `fS.hom`.
  - `NatIso.isIso_map_iff (e : F₁ ≅ F₂) (f) : IsIso (F₁.map f) ↔ IsIso (F₂.map f)` is the clean
    transport (avoids manual naturality/inv bookkeeping).
- **Blueprint:** new decl, not yet `\lean{}`-pinned. Plan agent: add a `lem:...` block for
  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_of_isIso_restrict` (the "locally-iso ⇒ iso" B-bridge) so
  `sync_leanok` can track it.

## `dual_isLocallyTrivial` (bridge C) — NOT ADDED (handoff)
- **Needed:** `IsLocallyTrivial L → IsLocallyTrivial (dual L)`. On a trivialising open `U`
  (`L.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), want `(dual L).restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`.
- **Crux (C1):** `(dual M).restrict f ≅ dual (M.restrict f)` for an open immersion `f` — the dual
  analogue of the CLOSED `tensorObj_restrict_iso` (L1822). Mirror its 4-step composite
  (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback` → `sheafificationCompPullback` → strip sheafification → presheaf
  residual). **The hard part is the presheaf step:** `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).obj
  (PresheafOfModules.dual A) ≅ PresheafOfModules.dual ((pullback φ).obj A)`, i.e. `restrictScalars`
  along the open-immersion ring iso commutes with the **bespoke** presheaf `dual` (= `internalHom(-,R)`,
  built iters 219–224 in this file). This needs lemmas relating `restrictScalars`/`pushforward β`
  to `internalHom`/`restrictionMap` section-by-section, using
  `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsEquivalenceOfRingEquiv` (`ChangeOfRings.lean:285`, additive `:325` +
  linear `:335`) to carry `Hom(-,-)` across the ring iso. This is a major mirror build (the bespoke
  dual was assembled over many iters); not attempted this iter.
- **Plus (C2):** `dual (𝒪_U) ≅ 𝒪_U` (dual of the structure sheaf is itself) at the sheaf level.

## A-bridge (SheafOfModules morphism descent) — NOT ADDED (handoff)
- **Needed:** glue compatible local morphisms (here: the canonical local trivialising isos
  `eᵢ : (L ⊗ dual L)|_{Uᵢ} ≅ 𝒪_{Uᵢ}`) into a global `g : tensorObj L (dual L) ⟶ 𝒪_X` whose
  restriction to each `Uᵢ` is `eᵢ.hom`. Then `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` (landed) finishes `IsIso g`.
- **Route (analogist ts226descent):** forget to the underlying ab-sheaf via `SheafOfModules.toSheaf`,
  glue with `CategoryTheory.Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom` / `sheafHomSectionsEquiv`
  (`Sites/SheafHom.lean:207,241`), then promote to `𝒪_X`-linear with `PresheafOfModules.homMk`
  (`Presheaf.lean:200`; R-linearity is a sectionwise equation, holds globally once on the cover).
- **Difficulty:** moderate-to-hard (~30–60 LOC). `sheafHomSectionsEquiv` works over the `Over X`
  category at the level of `Sheaf J (Type _)`; building the compatible family + the **overlap-cocycle
  agreement** of the `eᵢ` is the bounded-but-real content (NOT d.2). Not attempted this iter to avoid
  risking a sorry.

## Why I stopped — `Real progress`
- **1 axiom-clean declaration added:** `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isIso_of_isIso_restrict`
  (lines ~1936–1972), the cheapest of the three descent bridges (B), verified
  `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. It is the reusable "locally-iso ⇒ iso" supplement the
  assembly's final step consumes, and is directly in the form `IsLocallyTrivial` provides.
- The two remaining bridges (A morphism-descent, C dual-vs-restriction) are each genuine
  multi-iteration builds (C in particular mirrors the hard `tensorObj_restrict_iso` for the bespoke
  presheaf dual). Per the plan's explicit instruction, I handed off their precise next steps rather
  than pin a sorry into a new declaration. The B-connector is permanent forward progress and unblocks
  the final contraction once A + C land. No approach was identified-but-unattempted for B; for A/C the
  routes are scoped above and are the next iter's work.

## Self-review
1. Attempted every approach written in a comment? Yes for B (built it). A/C are scoped handoffs, not
   abandoned in-progress attempts.
2. Adjacent decls attempted with new infra? The adjacent pieces (A, C) are the handoff targets; both
   are large standalone builds, not quick wins reachable from B alone.
3. Informal agent / key: not needed — B was closed directly against Mathlib.
4. Skipped approach? No skipped tractable approach for B.
